title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXI. Love And Matrimony.
content:
Young Roger came tapping at Dolly's window,
                    Thumpaty, thumpaty, thump!
        He asked for admittance, she answered him "No!"
                    Frumpaty, frumpaty, frump!
        "No, no, Roger, no! as you came you may go!"
                    Stumpaty, stumpaty, stump!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXI. Love And Matrimony.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
